author: govinda dāsa
verses: sajani pekhalu varaja⋅kiśora piba⋅ite vadana⋅sudhākara⋅mādhurī mātala nayana⋅cakora mattama⋅ura⋅śikhaṇḍika⋅maṇḍita cūḍāye mālatī māla parimale māti pānti mata madhukara guñjare tāhi rasāla nīla jalada tanu bhāṅu madana dhanu nayana⋅kamala pañca bāṇa jara jara antara kulavatī⋅gaurava saṁśaya rahala parāṇe madana makara janu maṇimaya kuṇḍala ṭalamala dolata kāne hera⋅ite jaga⋅mana⋅mīna garāsaye govinda⋅dāsa paramāṇe
en_translation:
O friend! I saw the youthful Lord of Vraja! Drinking the sweetness of His moon-like face, the cakora birds of my eyes became intoxicated.

He is adorned with a beautiful peacock feather, and a jasmine garland rests upon His topknot. Intoxicated by its fragrance, rows of bees hum sweetly there.

His body is like a dark cloud, and His eyebrows break the bow of Cupid. His lotus eyes are like the five arrows of Cupid. My heart is agitated, and my very life is at stake.

Like the shark-shaped emblem of Cupid, jeweled earrings sway upon His ears. Seeing this, the fish of the world's mind is swallowed by His beauty; Govinda Dāsa testifies to this.
